mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate compresses the discount rate applied to future corporate cash flows, mechanically lifting equity valuations and producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A monthly decline in the unemployment rate below 5.5 percent signals a tightening labor market that accelerates wage growth and consumer spending, directly supporting corporate earnings and driving positive equity returns over a one-month horizon.
failure_condition: Forward returns in months where UNRATE drops below 5.5% are statistically indistinguishable from flat periods (p > 0.10), or the signal produces negative median returns over three consecutive years.

mechanism_narrative: Joint acceleration in housing starts and nonfarm payrolls confirms broad-based demand expansion, which lifts cyclical sector valuations and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 20-day window.
failure_condition: The combined signal fails to outperform a buy-and-hold benchmark in more than 55% of out-of-sample periods, or the interaction term in a multivariate regression yields a p-value greater than 0.15.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production exceeding 102 in a late-cycle expansion environment indicates capacity constraints that fuel inflationary pressures, prompting aggressive monetary tightening that compresses equity multiples and drives negative forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward returns remain positive in over 60% of instances where INDPRO breaches 102 during late-cycle phases, or the signal's directional accuracy drops below 45% in a 3-year walk-forward test.

mechanism_narrative: Sequential quarterly growth in real GDP directly scales aggregate corporate revenue expectations, producing a persistent positive drift in equity valuations that manifests as positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 5-day horizon.
failure_condition: The rolling 5-day forward return median is negative or statistically flat across all available quarterly GDP growth observations, or the signal's information coefficient falls below 0.02 over a 5-year period.

mechanism_narrative: Consumer price inflation breaching a 2.5 percent year-over-year threshold triggers expectations of hawkish Federal Reserve policy, raising the risk-free rate and discounting future earnings, which drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over a 20-day window.
failure_condition: Equity returns are positive in more than 50% of months where CPIAUCSL exceeds the 2.5% threshold, or the signal produces a negative Sharpe ratio over a 10-year backtest.

mechanism_narrative: The simultaneous presence of unemployment below 6.0 percent and rising consumer credit balances indicates sustained household spending power and credit availability, supporting forward earnings growth and driving positive equity returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to generate positive forward returns in more than 55% of out-of-sample periods, or the cross-product term in a factor model yields a t-statistic below 1.5.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid 10 percent month-over-month increase in housing starts during a post-shock recovery signals credit normalization and wealth effects, which rapidly lifts consumer discretionary valuations and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 20-day window.
failure_condition: Forward returns are negative or flat in over 60% of triggered episodes, or the signal's predictive power vanishes when controlling for FEDFUNDS changes in a multivariate regression.
